data.title: Hooker Furnishings outlines $25M cost reduction target while advancing Vietnam warehouse strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Hooker Furnishings Corporation (HOFT) Q1 2026

Management View

  • CEO Jeremy R. Hoff reported that "we continue to take significant and deliberate actions to stabilize the company, drive improved sales and deliver strong gross margins as we execute on our accelerated cost savings program." Hoff highlighted that the company achieved its eighth consecutive quarter of market share gains within Hooker's Legacy Brands and introduced new collections and the "Living Your Way" modular upholstery program, which had significant placements at the Spring High Point Market.

  • Hoff emphasized the expansion of a multipronged cost reduction strategy with a target of approximately $25 million in annualized savings by the next fiscal year, supported by the recent opening of a Vietnam warehouse facility aimed at reducing lead times from about 6 months to 4 to 6 weeks. "We see the Vietnam warehouse as a game changer and win-win for us and our customers," Hoff stated.

  • CFO C. Earl Armstrong reported, "Despite the decrease in net sales, we reduced our operating loss by $1.6 million or 31% to $3.6 million, reflecting the impact of cost reduction initiatives implemented in the second half of the prior fiscal year." Armstrong detailed operating expense reductions of $2.2 million and noted restructuring costs of $523,000, primarily in severance.

Outlook

  • Hoff stated, "In total, from the June 2024 start of our initiative, we anticipate reducing our total annual spend rate by approximately 25%. These savings alone will substantially improve profitability."

  • Armstrong projected, "In fiscal '26, Hooker expects to realize about $14 million in cost savings, net of offsets and special charges. By fiscal '27, Hooker expects to realize $25 million in net annualized savings through these phased initiatives, which should enhance profitability, operational efficiency and long-term order value."

  • Hoff outlined product and digital initiatives: "Key initiatives include the launch of our new Margaritaville licensing program, a best-in-class international warehouse that enables us to reduce domestic safety stock, preserve working capital and shortened lead times in our collective living whole home merchandising approach."

Financial Results

  • Armstrong reported consolidated net sales of $85.3 million for Q1 2026, a decrease of $8.3 million or 8.8% compared to the same period last year.

  • The net loss for the quarter was $3.1 million or $0.29 per diluted share, compared to the prior year's net loss of $4.1 million or $0.39 per diluted share. Gross margins improved by 190 basis points.

  • Armstrong highlighted that the company reduced operating expenses by $2.2 million, with Hooker Branded achieving breakeven, and Domestic Upholstery and Home Meridian reducing operating losses by 55% and 17%, respectively. Cash and cash equivalents stood at $18 million, with inventory levels at about $64 million at quarter end.

  • Armstrong noted, "Subsequent to the end of our first quarter, we paid down all outstanding borrowings on our revolving credit facility. As of yesterday, we had approximately $3 million in cash on hand with about $63 million in available borrowing capacity net of standby letters of credit."

Q&A

  • Anthony Chester Lebiedzinski, Sidoti & Company: Asked about shipment cadence and the impact of tariffs. Hoff responded, "I can definitely tell you that the cadence changed pretty drastically for us with the tariffs. It definitely affects, what we call the mega customer, which is really the HMI customer more so than the many customers we have that are very different on the Hooker Branded and Domestic Upholstery side of our business."

  • Lebiedzinski questioned gross margin impacts from discounting at Hooker Branded. Armstrong replied, "No. We don't have that in front of us, Anthony."

  • Lebiedzinski asked about drivers behind higher May orders at Legacy brands. Hoff explained, "We significantly broadened our merchandising strategy with collected living and the things we talked about. And it's starting -- we believe it's starting to have a positive effect."

  • Lebiedzinski probed on HMI trends and Memorial Day performance. Hoff said, "We're still seeing significant uncertainty due to tariffs because there really hasn't been other than we know the 10%, but... until there's clarification, that definitely hurts." On Memorial Day, "The overall sentiment that we found was that, it was relatively positive for most retailers for Memorial Day."

  • David Joseph Storms, Stonegate: Asked about cost savings cadence for the year. Armstrong replied, "With Phase 2 and these new cost initiatives, we'd expect probably a $250,000 net impact, positive impact in Q2, probably the opposite of that in Q3... We expect really to hit pretty significantly in Q4 to the tune of about $3.5 million."

  • Storms inquired about capital allocation priorities. Hoff responded, "Strengthening the balance sheet right now is priority one. And you're right on dividends being very high priority for us... our #1 focus is to make that balance sheet as strong as possible."

  • Storms asked about seasonality and momentum. Hoff said, "I believe the second half will be stronger than the first half... that's a historical trend that I'm fairly confident in. I'm not as confident to say that our May trend will continue... I'm hoping, yes."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts voiced cautious optimism, seeking clarity on margin impacts, cost savings pacing, and tariff uncertainties, with persistent questioning of sustainability and momentum.

  • Management expressed measured confidence in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently highlighting cost discipline and operational progress. Hoff repeatedly stated belief in the positive impact of merchandising and cost initiatives, with moments of hedging such as "I'm not as confident to say that our May trend will continue... I'm hoping, yes."

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone remained probing and cautious, while management's tone shifted slightly more confident regarding cost controls and strategic execution, though macro headwinds and tariff uncertainties were acknowledged as ongoing challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management increased the target cost savings initiative to $25 million in annualized savings by fiscal 2027, up from $18–$20 million discussed previously.

  • The Vietnam warehouse strategy advanced from planning to execution, with operational impacts now reported, including lead time reductions and positive customer feedback.

  • Financial results showed improved gross margins and a narrower net loss compared to the previous quarter, alongside a focus on cash preservation and debt reduction.

  • Guidance language reflected greater specificity and confidence in cost savings and operational streamlining, with continued caution on market conditions and tariff risks.

  • Analysts continued to focus on cost initiatives, margin sustainability, and macroeconomic headwinds, echoing themes from the prior quarter but with more specific questions on the pacing and impact of announced initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited persistent softness in the housing market, higher mortgage rates, and declining consumer sentiment as ongoing industry headwinds.

  • Tariff uncertainties, particularly regarding Vietnam, were highlighted as a key external risk, with Hoff stating, "Like everyone else, we are waiting to hear in July what the final tariff may be for Vietnam, where we source over 80% of our products."

  • Management outlined mitigation steps, including sharing tariff costs with source factories and implementing a 5% price increase, along with a phased cost reduction plan and supply chain improvements.

Final Takeaway

Hooker Furnishings management emphasized the company's ongoing transformation, highlighting accelerated cost reduction initiatives targeting $25 million in annualized savings, operational advances such as the Vietnam warehouse, and steady market share gains in core brands. While macroeconomic and tariff uncertainties continue to pressure demand, the company's leadership underscored its improved financial flexibility, operational streamlining, and readiness to capitalize on a market upturn, maintaining a disciplined focus on shareholder value and long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: J&J touts phase 1 data for bleximenib for acute myeloid leukemia
data.description:
• Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) said that new phase 1b data found that candidate bleximenib demonstrated antileukemic activity.
  • The selective menin inhibitor, in combination with Venclexta (venetoclax) and Vidaza (azacitidine), is under investigation for acute myeloid leukemia in individuals with certain genetic mutations.
  • The dose-finding study enrolled newly diagnosed, intensive chemo-ineligible AML, and relapsed or refractory AML patients.
  • J&J noted that bleximenib's safety profile was good, with no QTc prolongation signal, no events of Grade 3 or higher, and only three Grade 1 events at the recommended phase 2 dose.

data.title: U.S. Bank, Fiserv create integrated agent card issuance
data.description:
U.S. Bank (NYSE:USB) and Fiserv (NYSE:FI) formed a partnership that will integrate U.S. Bank's Elan credit card program into Fiserv's Credit Choice product with plans to introduce significant new capabilities later this year, the companies said on Thursday.

Credit Choice allows financial institutions to deliver a digital-first, branded agent-bank credit card program for consumer and small business card holders. As part of the alliance, Credit Choice, enhanced with Elan, will offer integrated digital technology that combines both debit and credit card account information for consumer and small business card holders within each financial institution's digital card management solution. 

As a result, customers can access credit and debit card information and servicing in one place. In addition, financial institutions will have easier access to the tools they need to onboard and service their cardholders, reducing friction and improving the ability to easily reach new potential card relationships, the companies said.

Users of the new platform will have access to Elan’s suite of agent-issuing capabilities. Fiserv’s (NYSE:FI) digital card solutions for consumer and business card and expense management will be integrated in the first half of 2026.

U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) stock slipped 0.5%, and Fiserv (FI) stock dipped 0.6% in Thursday morning trading.

data.title: Jet orders are expected at Paris Air Show amid tariffs, global turmoil
data.description:
Despite geopolitical uncertainty, rising tariffs, ongoing supply chain issues and large backlogs, aerospace manufacturers are preparing to present a unified front at the upcoming Paris Air Show, which runs June 16-20 at Le Bourget. The industry’s biggest players plan to spotlight new technologies, from artificial intelligence to autonomous systems, while unveiling major aircraft deals aimed at signaling resilience in the face of instability.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) is expected to make headlines early in the week with a pair of significant deals from Saudi carriers, highlighting the region’s growing air travel appetite, Reuters reported on Thursday. However, the European aircraft giant may face a last-minute challenge from Brazil’s Embraer (NYSE:ERJ), which is said to be vying for a sizable AirAsia order of around 100 A220-sized aircraft.

Boeing (NYSE:BA), while still in the game, is anticipated to have a lower profile during the show after announcing major Gulf orders during U.S. President Donald Trump’s recent diplomatic tour. Still, the American manufacturer is tipped to finalize an important fleet renewal deal with Royal Air Maroc.

Industry copes with volatility

Beyond the flashy announcements and aircraft displays, industry leaders are privately contending with an increasingly volatile operating environment. Civil aviation manufacturers and major defense contractors such as Lockheed Martin (NYSE:LMT) and Raytheon (NYSE:RTX) are closely monitoring the impact of trade tensions and military conflicts.

Although a tentative U.S.-China trade thaw has lifted some of the gloom, the specter of enduring 10% base tariffs lingers. Industry insiders say efforts are ongoing to secure exemptions, but many companies are adjusting to the likelihood that tariffs are here to stay, Reuters reported.

Defense spending surges

Russia’s war in Ukraine and heightened instability in the Middle East have prompted significant changes in defense planning. European countries including Germany, the UK, and Poland are boosting military budgets at levels not seen since the Cold War, with new investments focused on air defense, drones, and next-gen fighter platforms.

This year’s airshow will feature a wide range of unmanned aerial vehicles, including low-cost drones that have demonstrated battlefield value in Ukraine. These systems are becoming critical to modern warfare, offering flexible, lower-cost alternatives to crewed aircraft.

At the same time, Europe’s defense calculus is shifting. With confidence in U.S. security guarantees wavering under Trump-era foreign policy, several countries are re-evaluating their dependence on American systems in favor of building domestic capacity.

Sustainability goals at crossroads

Although environmental concerns will have a presence at the show, they’re unlikely to take center stage this year. Trump’s rollback of green initiatives has muted some of the momentum behind aerospace sustainability efforts.

Airlines recently reaffirmed their long-term goal of achieving net-zero emissions, but progress is being hampered by a lack of sustainable aviation fuel and delays in next-generation aircraft programs. High-profile projects, such as Airbus’ (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) hydrogen-powered aircraft, have slowed or stalled altogether.

For now, manufacturers argue that transitioning to more fuel-efficient models, offering roughly 15% savings over older jets, is the fastest route to reducing emissions. However, ongoing supply disruptions have slowed deliveries, and environmental advocates warn that aggressive sales strategies risk entrenching reliance on fossil fuels for decades to come.

As the nearly $1 trillion global aerospace sector converges on Paris, it does so at a moment defined by innovation, uncertainty, and an urgent need to adapt.

data.title: Goldman Sachs trims its 12-month U.S. recession probablity to 30%
data.description:
There is now a 30% chance of the U.S. economy facing a recession, as the effect of tariffs on the economy is now perceived to be smaller, according to Goldman Sachs. 

Goldman Sachs' earlier 12-month recession probability was at 35%.

Jan Hatzius, one of the lead analysts at Goldman Sachs, said there are three recent developments that point to a smaller effect of tariffs on the economy. 

The first development comes from the inflation prints, which, even though they offer only limited evidence, show a slightly lesser impact on consumer prices and hence, on real income and consumer spending. 

"In light of the more limited tariff effect this month and the confirmation that offsetting disinflationary momentum is still coming from other forces, especially the end of catch-up inflation in the shelter category, we have lowered our forecast for core PCE inflation, which we now expect to peak at 3.4% year-over-year, vs. 3.6% previously," Hatzius said in a note. 

The broader financial aspects have also eased back to roughly pre-tariff levels, Hatzius added. 

Measures of trade policy uncertainty have also moderated a bit after steps towards de-escalation have been reported. 

"We now estimate a roughly ¼pp smaller peak hit to GDP growth from tariffs than we did previously. We are therefore bumping up our 2025 Q4/Q4 GDP growth forecast from 1% previously to 1¼% and nudging down our peak unemployment rate forecast by 0.1pp to 4.4%," Hatzius said.

However, Hatzius said, they will not make any changes to their Federal Reserve forecast and will continue to expect the first of three normalization cuts in December, followed by two more in 2026 to a terminal rate of 3.5%-3.75%.

"An earlier cut is possible if the economy deteriorates more than we expect or if inflation continues to surprise to the downside, but we think that the peak summer tariff effects on the monthly inflation prints will most likely be too fresh for the FOMC to cut before December," Hatzius said.

data.title: Schrödinger slides after early-stage trial data for cancer therapy
data.description:
Shares of Schrödinger (NASDAQ:SDGR) fell on Thursday after the AI-focused biotech posted initial data from a Phase 1 trial for SGR-1505, an experimental therapy targeting relapsed/refractory B-cell malignancies.

Based on a May 13 data cut, Schrödinger (NASDAQ:SDGR) said ten patients out of 49 who were evaluable for safety in the dose-escalation study experienced treatment-emergent serious adverse events. There was a single case of a treatment-related serious adverse event.

While 43% of patients witnessed at least one treatment-related adverse event, SGR-1505 caused no dose-limiting toxicities or deaths, the company added.

As for efficacy, Schrödinger (NASDAQ:SDGR) reported a 22% overall response rate among 49 trial participants, 13 of whom had received the therapy for at least 120 days.

The ongoing trial is designed to test SGR-1505 as a single agent in patients with chronic lymphocytic leukemia and Waldenström macroglobulinemia, with its primary goal related to adverse events and dose-limiting toxicities.

Data was included in a poster presentation at the ongoing European Hematology Association Annual Congress.

data.title: Coinbase adds Democratic political strategist David Plouffe to advisory council
data.description:
Updates at 10:59 AM ET to reflect company confirmed report; updates stock and bitcoin price.

David Plouffe, known as the architect of Barack Obama's successful 2008 presidential campaign, has joined Coinbase Global's (NASDAQ:COIN) global advisory council, which already includes Donald Trump's former campaign manager and some former U.S. senators, to expand its political outreach, the company confirmed on Thursday.

The crypto exchange added the political strategist as Congress proceeds in crafting legislation to provide a framework to regulate digital assets. President Donald Trump has embraced crypto, vowing to make the U.S. the global crypto capital, in sharp contrast to his predecessor's more critical view of the sector.

Plouffe previously served on the global advisory board for Binance, the world's largest crypto exchange, before he joined Kamala Harris's presidential campaign as a senior adviser in August, the Associated Press reported.

Coinbase's (NASDAQ:COIN) advisory board acts as a  "sound board" on policy and business strategy, Chief Policy Officer Faryar Shirzad told the AP.

Other members of the advisory board include John Anzalone, founder of Impact Research Polling; former Defense Secretary Mark Esper; former Congresswoman Stephanie Murphy (D-FL); former Congressman Tim Ryan (D-OH); former Senator Patrick Toomey (R-PA); former U.S. Homeland Security Advisor Fran Townsend. David Urban: former mayor of Los Angeles Antonio Villaraigosa; former UK Chancellor of the Exchequer and First Secretary of State George Osborne; former mayor of Atlanta Keisha Lance Bottoms; former Congressman Kendrick Meek (D-FL); former Senator Kyrsten Sinema (D-AZ); former New York Fed President Bill Dudley; RNC strategist Chris LaCivita; and former Inter-American Development Bank President Luis Moreno.

Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Coinbase (COIN) stock fell 1.7% in Thursday morning trading, as bitcoin (BTC-USD) slid 0.9% to $107.7K.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: BNY appoints CEO Robin Vince to additional post of board chairman
data.description:
The Bank of New York Mellon (NYSE:BK) announced on Thursday the appointment of CEO Robin Vince to the additional position of board chairman.

Vince was unanimously elected by the board. Current Chairman Joe Echevarria has been appointed a lead independent director.

The new appointments are effective September 1.

data.title: Nvidia's GTC Paris conference reveals multiple new growth drivers: analysts
data.description:
Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) GTC Paris conference for developers, researchers and business leaders this week unfurled several new growth opportunities for the prolific producer of artificial intelligence processors, according to analysts.

Shares of Nvidia ticked up 1.5% during morning market action on Thursday. Its stock has tacked on 19% over the past month.

Morgan Stanley reiterated its Overweight rating and "Top Pick" status on Nvidia following developments from the conference. It has a $170 price target on the stock.

"With Nvidia announcing today plans that will require 'more than 3,000 exaflops of NVIDIA Blackwell compute'; that includes an 18k GB200 phase one deployment with Mistral in France that will expand to multiple locations next year, a 14k Blackwell development in the UK with Nebius and Nscale, along with others from partners like Domyn and with telcos Swisscom, Telefónica, Telenor, Fastweb and Orange," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Jospeh Moore, in an investor note. "Nvidia specifically will be building, 'the world's first industrial AI cloud for European manufacturers' featuring 10k Blackwell GPUs, and AI technology centers in Germany, Sweden, Italy, Spain, the U.K. and Finland focused on development and R&D."

Evercore ISI reiterated its Outperform rating and $190 price target, while maintaining Nvidia as a "top semi core holding."

"NVDA highlighted AI infrastructure projects from 5 European Telcos, 19 CSPs and 16 Supercomputing centers all for local compute consumption and using NVDA hardware," said Evercore analysts Mark Lipacis and Natalia Winkler, in an investor note. "NVDA expects EU government to support buildout of 20 AI factories, with a handful of them being gigafactories. Overall, NVDA expects 20 gigafactories ($40-50bn per gigawatt) over the coming years – all incremental to US CSPs."

data.title: National Fuel Gas raises dividend by ~4%
data.description:
• National Fuel Gas (NYSE:NFG) declared $0.535/share quarterly dividend, 3.9% increase from prior dividend of $0.515.
  • Forward yield 2.58%
  • Payable July 15; for shareholders of record June 30; ex-div June 30.
  • See NFG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
